name: AH-1 Cobra Attack Helicopter
prompt:
Vietnam War era Bell AH-1G Cobra attack helicopter. First dedicated attack helicopter, slim tandem-seat design.

Physical structure:
- Fuselage: very narrow/slim, tandem cockpit configuration
  - Chin turret: under nose, housing minigun or grenade launcher, cylindrical housing that rotates
  - Front cockpit (gunner): lower seat with flat panels of armored glass
  - Rear cockpit (pilot): raised seat behind gunner, higher canopy
  - Canopy: flat-panel greenhouse canopy over both seats (glassMaterial)
  - Engine: T53 turboshaft behind/above rear cockpit
  - Exhaust: on top, above tail boom junction
- Main rotor: two-blade, ~13.4m diameter
- Tail boom: slim cylindrical boom
  - Tail rotor: two-blade on right side
  - Vertical stabilizer: angled fin
  - Horizontal stabilizer: small wing at tail with end plates
- Stub wings: short fixed wings on each side, angled down
  - Inner pylons: can carry rockets, missiles, or gun pods
  - Outer pylons: same
  - Show with 2.75" rocket pods (7-tube or 19-tube clusters)
- Landing skids: tubular, narrow stance matching slim fuselage

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark weapons/pylons (0x333333), canopy glass (0x88aaaa), exhaust dark (0x2a2a2a)
Orientation: On skids, nose along +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~13.5m long, ~4.1m tall)`,
    animations: `Main rotor: Y-axis spin, 1s revolution
Tail rotor: X-axis spin, 0.3s revolution
Chin turret: slow Y-axis sweep, 4s cycle sweeping 60 degrees`
  },
  {
    name: 'AC-47 Spooky Gunship',
    slug: 'ac47-spooky',
    category: 'vehicles/aircraft',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 8000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Douglas AC-47 "Spooky" / "Puff the Magic Dragon" gunship. Modified C-47 Skytrain with side-firing miniguns.

Physical structure:
- Fuselage: classic DC-3/C-47 cylindrical fuselage, ~19.4m long
  - Nose: rounded nose with cockpit windshield panels (5-6 glass panels)
  - Passenger windows: row of small round/rectangular windows along fuselage
  - Cargo door: large door on left side (port), shown open
  - Gun ports: 3 minigun positions visible through left side openings
- Wings: low-wing monoplane, distinctive tapered wings
  - Wing span: ~28.9m (each wing ~12m from fuselage)
  - Wing: tapered, rounded tips
  - No wing-mounted weapons
- Engines: two Pratt & Whitney R-1830 radial engines
  - Engine nacelles on wing leading edges
  - Three-blade propellers on each engine
- Tail section:
  - Horizontal stabilizer: rectangular, mounted on fuselage
  - Vertical stabilizer: single tall fin with rudder
  - Tailwheel: small wheel at very rear
- Landing gear: taildragger configuration
  - Main gear: two fixed main wheels under engine nacelles (or retracted)
  - Tail wheel
- Miniguns (3): SUU-11/A minigun pods visible inside/through left side doors
  - Show as cylindrical barrel clusters pointing out left side

Colors: OD green/gray (0x556B2F), silver/aluminum (0x999999 for unpainted areas), propellers dark (0x333333), glass cockpit (0x88aaaa), engine cowls (0x444444), gun metal (0x2a2a2a)
Orientation: On landing gear, nose along +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (or 1/2 scale to ~10m for game)`,
    animations: `Both propellers: spin on Z axis (along engine direction), 0.5s revolution
Note: Can also animate as flying (slight bobbing)`
  },
  {
    name: 'ZPU-4 Anti-Aircraft Gun',
    slug: 'zpu4-aa',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Soviet ZPU-4 quad anti-aircraft gun. Primary NVA low-altitude air defense weapon.

Physical structure:
- Mount: central pedestal/turntable mount on base
  - Base: flat circular base with leveling jacks
  - Turntable: rotates 360 degrees in azimuth
- Gun cradle: holds 4 x 14.5mm KPV heavy machine gun barrels
  - Barrels arranged in 2x2 square pattern
  - Each barrel: long (~1.5m), thin, with flash hider
  - Shared receiver housing connecting all 4 barrels
  - Elevates on trunnions (can tilt up for anti-aircraft)
- Ammunition: 4 drum magazines, one per barrel
  - Mounted on top/sides of receiver
  - Cylindrical drums
- Sight: mechanical ring sight or optical sight on top
- Seat: gunner's seat behind the cradle, simple metal chair
- Wheels: two rubber-tired wheels on axle for towing (folded up in firing position)
- Trail legs: two outrigger legs folded down for stability
- Shoulder rests: control handles for traversing

Colors: Dark military green (0x3d4a2a), barrel metal (0x333338), rubber wheels (0x222222), brass ammo (0xb5a642)
Orientation: Facing +Z, on ground, barrels slightly elevated
Scale: Real-world meters (~3m deployed width, ~1.5m tall)`,
    animations: `Barrel elevation: cradle tilts up 45 degrees and back over 4 seconds
Turret traverse: base rotates 30 degrees left/right over 6 seconds`
  },
  {
    name: '37mm Anti-Aircraft Gun',
    slug: '37mm-aa',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Soviet 61-K 37mm automatic anti-aircraft cannon. Medium-altitude NVA air defense.

Physical structure:
- Barrel: single long 37mm barrel (~2.5m), with muzzle brake (two-port)
  - Flash hider at muzzle
  - Barrel jacket/sleeve around breech area
- Receiver: large breech/receiver mechanism
  - Ammunition clips feed from top (5-round clips)
  - Ejection port on side
- Cradle: gun mounts on elevation cradle
  - Trunnions for elevation (-5 to +85 degrees)
  - Balance spring housing
- Turntable: 360-degree traverse mount
  - Central pedestal/post
  - Platform base
- Seats: two seats (gunner and loader), one each side
  - Shoulder rests with control wheels
  - Elevation handwheel
  - Traverse handwheel
- Legs: 4 outrigger stabilizer legs, deployed in X pattern
  - Ground pads at ends
- Shield: small curved armor shield in front of gunner position (optional)
- Ammunition box: ammo can near loader position

Colors: Military dark green (0x3d4a2a), gun steel (0x3a3a3a), darker metal (0x2a2a2a), seats (0x555555)
Orientation: Barrel pointing +Z, elevated ~30 degrees, deployed on ground
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m long including barrel, ~2m tall in firing position)`,
    animations: `Barrel elevation: elevates from 30 to 80 degrees over 3 seconds, then back`
  },
  {
    name: 'Water Buffalo',
    slug: 'water-buffalo',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'character',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnamese water buffalo (Bubalus bubalis). Domestic work animal seen throughout Vietnam, used for rice paddy cultivation.

Physical structure (build from primitive shapes):
- Body: large barrel-shaped torso using elongated box or cylinder
  - ~2m long, ~1.2m wide at belly, muscular and heavy
  - Slight hump at shoulders
- Head: wide, blocky head with broad muzzle
  - Mounted on short thick neck
  - Nostrils: wide
  - Eyes: small, on sides of head
- Horns: two large curved horns sweeping backward from top of head
  - Wide spread, ~0.8m tip to tip
  - Use curved cylinder or cone shapes
  - Horns curve back and slightly up
- Legs: 4 stocky legs, short relative to body mass
  - Front legs: slightly thicker
  - Hooves: dark, wide
  - Use cylinders for legs
- Tail: thin, medium length, hangs down with tuft at end
- Overall: very stocky, heavy build, low center of gravity
- Skin: mostly hairless, dark gray/brown

Colors: Dark charcoal gray hide (0x3a3535), slightly lighter underbelly (0x4a4545), dark horns (0x2a2520), black hooves (0x1a1a1a), pink inner muzzle (0x8a5a5a)
Orientation: Standing on 4 legs at Y=0, facing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~2.5m long, ~1.5m tall at shoulder)`,
    animations: `Idle breathing: subtle body bob, 3s cycle, 0.03m height
Tail swish: tail pivot rotates Z-axis +/-15 degrees, 2s cycle
Head nod: head pivot rotates X-axis +/-5 degrees, 4s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Macaque Monkey',
    slug: 'macaque',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'character',
    budget: 1500,
    prompt: `Long-tailed macaque (Macaca fascicularis). Common throughout Vietnam's jungles, often seen near villages and camps.

Physical structure (simplified, ~0.5m body length):
- Body: small rounded torso, slightly hunched
  - Chest area, belly slightly rounded
- Head: round head with flat face
  - Muzzle: slight protruding snout
  - Eyes: forward-facing (two small spheres or indentations)
  - Ears: small rounded ears on sides
- Arms: two arms, thin, long relative to body
  - Hands: simplified block hands
  - Arms slightly bent at elbows
- Legs: two legs, bent in sitting/crouching pose
  - Feet: flat
- Tail: long thin tail, ~0.5m, curves upward then drapes
  - Use thin cylinder or series of connected parts
- Overall: small, agile-looking primate

Colors: Gray-brown fur (0x8a7d6a), lighter belly/face (0xb5a890), pink face skin (0xc8a090), dark eyes (0x222222)
Orientation: Sitting on ground, facing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~0.5m body, ~0.3m head height when sitting = ~0.4m total sitting)`,
    animations: `Idle: subtle body sway, 2s cycle
Head look: head pivot rotates Y-axis +/-20 degrees, 3s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Punji Stake Trap',
    slug: 'punji-trap',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'prop',
    budget: 500,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Viet Cong punji stake trap. Camouflaged pit trap with sharpened bamboo stakes.

Physical structure:
- Pit: rectangular hole in ground, ~0.6m x 0.6m, ~0.5m deep
  - Dirt walls (dark brown inner walls)
  - Show as a recessed box below ground level
- Stakes: 6-8 sharpened bamboo stakes pointing upward from pit floor
  - Each stake: thin cone, ~0.3m tall, ~0.02m base diameter
  - Slightly varying heights and angles (not perfectly uniform)
  - Arranged in irregular cluster at pit bottom
- Camouflage cover: thin layer of leaves/sticks over pit (shown partially broken/displaced)
  - Flat irregular plane with leaf-green color, partially covering pit
  - Some cover fallen into pit (a few small flat pieces among stakes)
- Ground surface: dirt/jungle floor level at Y=0
  - Pit is below surface

Colors: Bamboo stakes (0xc8a850), dirt pit walls (0x5a4030), ground surface (0x4a5a2a), leaf cover (0x3a5a1a), fallen debris (0x5a6a2a)
Orientation: Pit in ground, cover breaks toward +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~0.6m square, ~0.5m deep)
